27.text: Title This Law may be cited as the “Personal Property Law 2005".

29.text: This Law is made by the Ruler of Dubai.

31.text: This Law applies in the jurisdiction of the DIFC.

33.text: This Law is enacted on the date specified in the Enactment Notice in respect of this Law.

35.text: This Law comes into force on the date specified in the Enactment Notice in respect of this Law.

38.text: (a) interpretative provisions which apply to the Law; and (b) a list of defined terms used in the Law.

44.text: Unless stated otherwise in this Law, this Law applies to any property other than real property, regardless of whether such property is capable of being owned or transferred under the laws of any other...

48.text: 8. General rule as to transfers of title to property

49.text:
(1) Where there is a consensual transfer of property, title is transferred to the transferee at such time as the transferor and the transferee mutually intend it to be transferred.   
(2) For the purp...

53.text: (1) Where property is transferred by a person who is not its owner, and who does not transfer it under the authority or with the consent of the owner, the transferee acquires no better title to the pr...

55.text: (1) Article 8 to 10 of this Law shall not apply to Digital Assets.

59.text: (2) General rules as to title and transfer of title of Digital Assets shall be determined by Part 3 of the Digital Assets law.

61.text: (1) Subject to Articles 10 and 14 of this Law, an innocent transferee of:

65.text: (4) This Article 12 shall not apply to a transfer of Money, Financial Collateral, Negotiable Instrument or Negotiable Document (which includes such property that is also a Digital Asset).

72.text: 14. Transfer of property subject to a Security Right

73.text: The circumstances under which a transferee of title to property takes free of a Security Right is determined by the Law of Security.

75.text: This Part is subject to the provisions of Parts 5 and 6.

91.text: (3) The judgement or decree may be unconditional, or on such terms and conditions as to damages, payment of the price and otherwise as seem just to the Court.
